title: One phone rule that changes who wins your morning
caption:
You give it the first minutes of your day.

Before you've prayed. Before you've thought. Before you've decided anything. The feed walks in before God does — and it sets the tone for everything that follows.

One rule: give God the first word. Open the Word before you open your phone. Ten minutes of Scripture before the first notification changes whose voice is loudest in your head all morning.

And name what the scroll actually takes from you. An hour on the feed before 9 AM isn't neutral — it's borrowed from prayer, from silence, from the clarity your decisions need. Name the trade once and you can't unknow it.

Willpower doesn't beat the algorithm — friction does. Charge the phone in a different room. The half-second you spend going to get it breaks the automatic reach. Physical distance is a spiritual practice.

Don't just break the habit. Replace it. "Set your mind on things above." — Colossians 3:2. That's not a mood. It's a practice.

Set it up tonight before you go to sleep.

—

Comment "morning" and I'll send you the link to the app.
